codeReview.summary: The memory-memory-blocks feature is well-implemented with no blocking issues. Created blocks.rs module with MemoryBlockLoader trait, DefaultBlockLoader, ContextAssembler, and helper functions. All 7 tests pass, clippy is clean.

issues.description: create_project_block always uses label 'project:architecture' regardless of whether content is about architecture or conventions. The content format includes both architecture AND conventions sections, but the label only reflects the architecture part.
issues.suggestedFix: Consider using a more generic label like 'project:info' since the content covers both architecture and conventions. Alternatively, add a label parameter to the function to allow callers to specify the label.

sharedStateObservations.observation: The worker expected a 'rust-worker' skill but features.json specifies skillName 'Rust'. The transcript shows the worker invoked the Rust skill instead after finding rust-worker didn't exist. The actual available skill is 'Rust' not 'rust-worker'.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: Transcript shows: 'Skill "rust-worker" not found' followed by successful invocation of 'Rust' skill. Handoff.skillFeedback.followedProcedure is false with deviation: 'rust-worker skill was not found, so I invoked the Rust skill instead'.

sharedStateObservations.observation: The Rust skill documentation correctly guided the worker on async_trait::async_trait usage and other Rust patterns. No issues with the skill content itself.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: Worker successfully used #[tokio::test] for async tests, Arc<dyn Trait> for dynamic dispatch, and followed error handling patterns as documented in the Rust skill.

sharedStateObservations.observation: The worker discovered and correctly used the pattern for creating MemoryEntry with all required fields (is_stale, last_reinforcement, provenance_context, etc). This pattern is not explicitly documented in AGENTS.md but is consistent with existing code in lib.rs.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: blocks.rs lines 211-224, 237-250, 263-276 show correct MemoryEntry construction with all fields. The existing tests in lib.rs use the same pattern.

summary: Feature passes scrutiny. Implementation correctly covers all expected behavior: Project/User/Task memory blocks with auto-loading via MemoryBlockLoader trait and context assembly via ContextAssembler. Build succeeds, 7 tests pass, clippy clean. One non-blocking label naming suggestion. Worker followed Rust skill guidance correctly after skill name mismatch.
